specification.topic-statement: The terminal UI is composed of seven named layers that render specific content to distinct regions of the terminal screen, stacking from top to bottom to form the complete interactive display.

scope.in-scope: The identity, content, visibility rules, and height of each of the seven layers, and their spatial relationship to each other.
scope.boundary: How layers are composited, clipped to the viewport, and written to the terminal in a single atomic pass is covered by the layer rendering specification.
How the text lines stored in the scrollback buffer are structured and how physical row counts are computed is covered by the scrollback buffer specification.

behavior.stack-order:
The seven layers occupy the terminal from top to bottom in the following fixed order:
1. Banner 2. Scrollback 3. Separator 4. Input 5. Spinner 6. Status 7. Completion
The scrollback region dominates the middle of the terminal and expands or contracts to fill whatever vertical space is not consumed by the other layers.
behavior.banner-layer:
The banner is an optional decorative header occupying exactly 6 rows. It displays a stylized ASCII art face with the application name and version. The visual elements use 256-color styling: cyan eyes with gold pupils, a coral smile, dim gray border lines, soft blue version text, and a light-gray tagline. The top and bottom of the banner are framed by double horizontal lines.
The banner contains no dynamic content — it does not reflect agent state or user input. It is purely decorative.
When hidden, the 6 rows it would occupy become available to the scrollback layer.
behavior.scrollback-layer:
The scrollback layer is always visible and occupies all vertical space not claimed by other layers. It renders the agent's conversation history: user messages, LLM responses, tool calls and results, command output, and system events, in chronological order.
The layer maintains a layout cache mapping logical scrollback lines to physical screen rows, accounting for text wrapping at the terminal width. It renders only the subset of rows that fall within the viewport (as determined by the current scroll offset). Newlines embedded within a scrollback line are rendered with ANSI clear-to-end-of-line sequences to ensure correct display across wrapped lines.
When the terminal is resized, the layout cache is recomputed using the pre-calculated per-segment widths stored with each line, without rescanning the text.
behavior.separator-layer:
The separator is an optional 1-row horizontal divider composed of light box-drawing characters that marks the boundary between the scrollback and the input area.
The separator can optionally embed two kinds of overlay information:
1. **Navigation context**: abbreviated UUIDs of the current agent's parent, previous sibling, next sibling, and most recent child, shown with directional arrow characters. If a direction has no agent (for example, the root agent has no parent), a dim dash is shown instead. 2. **Debug statistics**: scroll offset, viewport position, viewport height, document height, computed scrollback row count, and last render time in milliseconds or microseconds.
Both overlays are conditional on external configuration; neither appears in normal production use.
behavior.input-layer:
The input layer displays the text currently being typed or edited by the user. Its height is dynamic: it occupies the minimum number of rows required to display the full input text at the current terminal width, with a minimum of 1 row (to provide a cursor position even when the buffer is empty).
Newlines in the input text are converted to ANSI clear-to-end-of-line sequences before rendering, matching the behavior used in the scrollback layer.
The cursor position within the input layer is the cursor byte offset translated to a terminal row and column using the rendering context's cursor position calculation.
behavior.spinner-layer:
The spinner is a 1-row indicator that appears while the agent is waiting for an LLM response. It cycles through ten Braille dot-pattern frames to produce a subtle animation indicating ongoing activity. The animation advances every 120 milliseconds. A fixed label follows the animation frame.
The spinner is hidden when the agent is idle (not awaiting a response).
behavior.status-layer:
The status layer is a fixed 2-row footer. The first row is a light horizontal line separator. The second row displays the current agent's configured model name and thinking level, separated by a forward slash. If no model is configured, a placeholder is shown in soft blue.
The status layer always occupies its 2 rows; it is never hidden.
behavior.completion-layer:
The completion layer is an optional dropdown that appears at the bottom of the screen when a tab-completion session is active. It renders one row per candidate. Each row shows the command name followed by its description. The currently highlighted candidate is rendered with reverse video and bold styling to distinguish it from the others. Each row is padded to the terminal width to clear any previously rendered content.
The layer is hidden when no completion session is active. When visible, its height equals the number of available candidates.
